title: The Mystery of the Missing Mango
synopsis: When Lily visits her grandmother's farm in Kenya, she discovers that someone has been stealing mangoes from the special tree. With help from friendly savanna animals, she follows clues to solve the mystery and discovers an unexpected friend who just needed help.

pages.text: Lily loved visiting Grandma Kesi's farm in Kenya. The best part was the big mango tree in the yard. Its branches were heavy with sweet, orange fruit. Every morning, Lily would pick one perfect mango for breakfast. She loved the way the juice ran down her chin when she took big bites.

illustration.description: A young girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes stands under a large mango tree painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes. The tree is full of orange mangoes against warm savanna colors with golden grass and an orange sunset in the background. Bold confident strokes show the textured painterly style.
illustration.alt: Lily standing under a mango tree full of fruit

pages.text: But one morning, something was wrong. Lily ran to the mango tree, ready for her sweet breakfast. She looked up at the branches. Then she looked again. All the ripe mangoes were gone! Only green ones were left hanging high above her head. 'Grandma!' Lily called. 'Someone took all our mangoes!'

illustration.description: A girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes looks up at a mango tree with surprised expression. The gouache painting shows thick brushstrokes in warm savanna colors, with only green unripe mangoes on the tree. Golden grass surrounds the scene with textured painterly orange sunset colors and bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Lily looking up at the mango tree with only green fruit remaining

pages.text: Grandma Kesi came outside. She put her arm around Lily's shoulders. 'This is strange,' she said. 'Let's look for clues like real detectives.' They walked around the tree together. Lily bent down and studied the ground carefully. She wanted to solve this mystery.

illustration.description: A girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes crouches down examining the ground near a mango tree. An elderly woman stands beside her. The gouache painting uses thick brushstrokes and warm savanna colors with golden grass, textured painterly style, and bold confident strokes showing an orange sunset background.
illustration.alt: Lily and her grandmother searching for clues around the mango tree

pages.text: Near the trunk of the tree, Lily found something interesting. There were small round holes in the soft dirt. They looked like footprints, but not from people. 'Look, Grandma!' Lily pointed at the marks. 'These must be from the mango thief!' The holes were perfectly round and about the size of bottle caps.

illustration.description: A close-up gouache painting showing a girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes pointing at small round holes in the dirt. The painting uses thick brushstrokes and warm savanna colors with golden grass visible, textured painterly style with bold confident strokes creating an expressive scene.
illustration.alt: Lily pointing at mysterious round footprints in the dirt

pages.text: A colorful bird landed on a nearby branch. 'Hello, little detective,' chirped the lilac-breasted roller. 'I saw what happened to your mangoes!' Lily's eyes grew wide. She had never talked to a bird before. 'You can help us?' she asked excitedly. The bird nodded its beautiful blue and purple head.

illustration.description: A girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes stands looking up at a vibrant lilac-breasted roller bird. The gouache painting shows thick brushstrokes and warm savanna colors with expressive animals rendered in textured painterly style. Golden grass and orange sunset colors frame the scene with bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Lily talking to a colorful lilac-breasted roller bird

pages.text: The bird told Lily to follow the round footprints. They led away from the tree, across the golden grass, toward the watering hole. A friendly zebra trotted over to join them. 'I know who made those tracks,' the zebra said with a swish of her black and white tail. 'But you might be surprised when you find out!'

illustration.description: A girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes walks through golden grass following footprints, accompanied by a colorful bird flying overhead and an expressive zebra painted in gouache. The scene shows thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, textured painterly style with orange sunset and bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Lily following the trail with her new animal friends

pages.text: The trail led to a big baobab tree near the water. Lily heard soft munching sounds coming from behind the wide trunk. She tiptoed closer, with the bird and zebra following quietly. What she saw made her gasp. A baby hippo was sitting there, surrounded by mango peels and enjoying the last piece of fruit.

illustration.description: A girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes peers around a large baobab tree painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes. A small hippo sits eating mangoes in warm savanna colors. The textured painterly style shows golden grass and orange sunset with expressive animals and bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Lily discovering a baby hippo eating mangoes behind a baobab tree

pages.text: The baby hippo looked up with big, sad eyes. Orange mango juice covered his round snout. 'I'm sorry,' he said in a tiny voice. 'I was so hungry, and the mangoes smelled so good. My mama went to find food, but she's been gone for three days. I didn't know what else to do.' Lily's heart melted. The little hippo wasn't a thief - he was just scared and hungry.

illustration.description: A close-up gouache painting of a girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes kneeling beside a sad baby hippo with mango juice on its face. The painting uses thick brushstrokes and warm savanna colors with expressive animals rendered in textured painterly style with golden grass and bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Lily comforting the sad baby hippo covered in mango juice

pages.text: Lily ran back to get Grandma Kesi. Together, they brought the baby hippo fresh water and more mangoes. 'You can stay here until your mama comes back,' Lily told him gently. They made a soft bed of grass near the watering hole. The zebra promised to keep watch for the mama hippo.

illustration.description: A girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes and an elderly woman offer mangoes and water to a baby hippo. The gouache painting shows thick brushstrokes and warm savanna colors with a zebra watching nearby. The textured painterly style uses golden grass, orange sunset, and bold confident strokes with expressive animals.
illustration.alt: Lily, her grandmother, and animal friends caring for the baby hippo

pages.text: Two days later, they heard a loud, happy grunt. Mama hippo had returned! She nuzzled her baby and thanked Lily for taking such good care of him. From that day on, the hippo family visited the farm every week. Lily always saved the sweetest mangoes for her new friend. The mystery was solved, and she had gained the best treasure of all - a wonderful friendship.

illustration.description: A joyful gouache painting showing a girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes standing with a baby hippo and its large mother near the mango tree. The scene uses thick brushstrokes and warm savanna colors with golden grass, orange sunset, textured painterly style, expressive animals, and bold confident strokes creating a heartwarming scene.
illustration.alt: Lily celebrating with the reunited hippo family under the mango tree

metadata.illustrationStyle: gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes

questions.question: What was missing from the mango tree?
questions.explanation: Lily discovered that all the ripe, orange mangoes were gone, leaving only the green unripe ones on the tree.

questions.question: What clues did Lily find on the ground?
questions.explanation: Lily found small, round holes in the soft dirt that looked like footprints from the mango thief.

questions.question: Which animals helped Lily solve the mystery?
questions.explanation: A lilac-breasted roller bird and a friendly zebra both helped Lily follow the trail and solve the mystery.

questions.question: Who was taking the mangoes?
questions.explanation: The mystery was solved when Lily found a baby hippo who was hungry and alone while his mama was away looking for food.

questions.question: How did the story end?
choices: Lily became friends with the hippo family
Lily built a fence around the tree
questions.explanation: The mama hippo returned, and Lily gained wonderful new friends who visited the farm every week to share mangoes.
